mission.title: strimzi: Support mounting a volume to the pod
mission.description: Support mounting a volume to the pod. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify strimzi feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your strimzi deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=strimzi
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Support mounting a volume to the pod

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant strimzi resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When analyzing out-of-memory problems happening in JVM, it helps to collect and look at the heap dump (see [DBZ-1104](https://issues.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support mounting a volume to the pod
steps.description:
This PR introduces enhancements to the Strim
```yaml
storage:
      type: persistent-claim
      size: 1Gi
      class: rook-cephfs
      deleteClaim: false
      mountPath: /mnt/shared
    template:
      pod:
        securityContext:
          runAsNonRoot: false
          fsGroup: 1001
```

steps.title: Confirm Support mounting a volume to the pod is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: This issue was resolved by applying the fix from the linked PR. The root cause was identified and addressed by the community.

storage:
      type: persistent-claim
      size: 1Gi
      class: rook-cephfs
      deleteClaim: false
      mountPath: /mnt/shared
    template:

          fsGroup: 1001

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with strimzi installed or the issue environment reproducible.
